description: 185 - 15-04-2025 - Meeting between Peter Mandelson and Graeme Biggar (Director General of the National Crime Agency)
text:
General of the National Crime Agency)

Subject: [OS] Readout - DG NCA and HMA Washington intro 15.04.25
23 Apr 2025, 08:13

Office of the DG
Personal

to BIGGAR, Graeme, Robert Jones - DG Operations, JCS

, DGs Private Offices Only, NCAS
Engagement, Director Intel Private Office, Director Strategy Private Office

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Dear Graeme,

Jcs

With many thanks to ““*, please see below a readout of your H/S introductory call

with HMA Lord Peter Mandelson on 15" April.
Attendees

Graeme Biggar - DG NCA
Lord Peter Mandelson - HMA Washington
JCS = —Regional Manager, North America
JCS — - Head of Operations North America and The Caribbean

JCS = —Senior Manager, North America and Caribbean
JCS - DGPO
Readout

Following brief introductions, HMA asked about how the National Crime Agency fits
into the wider LE/Policing and whether there was tension between the NCA and local

Police forces. You gave a full explanation about the UK LE landscape and how the

534

NCA fits in, being the national lead on SOC and setting the strategic direction across 
Policing; you also covered his concern about possible tensions. 
Data Access / online activity / combating the use of data to facilitate crime was on 
HMA’s mind.  You explained the change we have seen in criminality and how it has 
moved ‘online’, particularly fraud, cybercrime and CSAE, but you also pointed out 
how almost all other crime has a data angle, due to phone/device usage. 
You spoke in detail about the UKUSDAA 
. At HMA’s request, you explained the 
DAA and E2EE to him in detail,  highlighting the difference between lawful access 
and content moderation, but how both were impacted by E2EE.  You informed HMA 
about our ILO in NCMEC and how NCMEC operates - millions of reports from tech 
companies being triaged via NCMEC, resulting in global referrals. You informed HMA 
that these referrals lead to 800 arrests per month and 1000 children being 
safeguarded as a result. HMA was visibly shocked by the figures you quoted. You 
went on to explain that we estimate that between 1.3 and 1.6% of adults in the UK 
(approximately 800,000) pose a serious sexual risk to children and how tech 
companies signpost us to this material, for us to act upon, hopefully preventing 
contact abuse. 
AP 1: You suggested HMA visited NCMEC, which he was extremely keen to do, and 
I agreed to arrange (16/4 update: visit confirmed for 13/5).  
HMA enquired about your ‘general’ counterparts, and you spoke about the FBI, DEA, 
HSI & USSS. You talked about the opportunities for collaboration even with the 
change in priority to drugs/OIC on the SW border. You spoke about cyber and online 
crimes, detailing our tremendous success with the use of US sanctions tools to 
tackle Russian ransomware attacks. 
 
HMA was due to meet with META that afternoon and sought your views.  You 
discussed how valuable they were particularly in relation to fraud and that META was 
the biggest contributor to NCMEC cyber tips, but you expressed concern about the 
impact of E2EE. 
 
***
***
***
535


HMA asked for any final information he should be aware of, and you briefly explained 
our footprint in the USA and how our Miami office also covers the Caribbean – 
 
Overall, a very positive first meeting, with HMA’s interest and engagement increasing 
as he learned more about the NCA and our mission, setting the tone for additional 
engagement at post. 
Thanks, 
 
 
Staff Officer to Director General 
National Crime Agency 
 
JCS
Personal
***
536
